events.title: Claudette Club | AI Build Day
events.description:
Build Your AI Team: Claudette Club

A monthly build day for female founders.

Imagine having an executive assistant, content strategist, community manager, or operations coordinator supporting your business without hiring anyone.

At Claudette Club, we'll help you build custom AI teammates inside Claude that save time, reduce overwhelm, and help you get more done.

This is a hands-on working session where you'll learn, build, and leave with AI tools you can start using right away.

You'll:

✨ Learn how to think in AI teammates

✨ Build your first (or next) AI teammate

✨ Create custom instructions and knowledge files

✨ Get support and feedback as you build

✨ Connect with other female founders

Bring:

💻 Your laptop

💡 A business challenge

Come build with us.

About Creative Collective
Creative Collective is a female founder house designed to help women build and grow their businesses through community, resources, programming, and support. It’s a space for founders to connect, take action, and grow alongside women who are building too. @creativecollectiveatx

events.title: [NOW AT BREWTORIUM] ペラペラNight with AJC 🌸 日本語・英語交流会 - Japanese・English Language Exchange
events.description:
<日本語は英語の後に続きます>

About This Event

AJC’s weekly Japanese–English language exchange, ぺらぺらナイト, is a relaxed and welcoming space for language learners in Austin.

This is not a formal class or structured lesson. Instead, we focus on natural conversations and comfortable language practice in a supportive environment.

All Japanese and English levels are welcome — from complete beginners to native speakers. You’re free to speak as much as you like, or simply listen and enjoy the atmosphere.

Who This Event Is For

Everyone is welcome!  This language exchange may be especially enjoyable for those who:

Want to practice Japanese or English in a casual setting

Are interested in Japanese, American, Texas, Austin, or other cultures

Prefer relaxed conversation over formal study

Feel nervous speaking but would like a safe place to listen and learn

What You Can Expect

A friendly and comfortable learning environment

Conversations at your own pace

Encouragement and patience from fellow learners

Cultural exchange and shared language-learning experiences

There is no pressure to speak if you’re not ready.
If speaking feels difficult, you are welcome to politely listen and observe.

Everyone is learning at their own pace.
Kindness, patience, and support are what make this community special.

Schedule (Tentative)

Every Tuesday from 6:00-8:00 PM
6:00–6:30 PM: Sign in and casual conversation
6:30–7:00 PM: Japanese conversation
7:00–7:30 PM: English conversation
After 7:30 PM: Free conversation (English or Japanese)

※ You’re welcome to join late or leave early.

One More Thing

Many participants will be attending for the first time.
Please feel free to come as you are — no preparation needed.

While this is a FREE event, we kindly ask attendees to support our venue by ordering food or a drink during the meetup. Your support helps us continue offering community events like this.

Notes

Tickets: Advance ticket registration is required due to venue capacity. Participants who attend without registration may be asked for their email addresses. Your personal information is handled under the AJC Privacy Policy.

Cancellations: Please cancel through the event page if your plans change.

Photography: Photos may be taken during the event and used on AJC’s website or social media. If you do not wish to appear in photos, please inform AJC staff in advance.

Safety: AJC is not responsible for theft, injuries, or other incidents that occur during the event.

No Solicitation: Unauthorized sales or solicitation are not allowed. Anyone disrupting the safe and welcoming environment may be asked to leave or restricted from future events.

We look forward to seeing you at ぺらぺらナイト!

Please contact Jan for any questions about this event.

*******************

イベントについて

AJCランゲージエクスチェンジ 「ぺらぺらナイト」 は、オースティンで日本語や英語を学ぶ皆さんのための、リラックスした温かい交流の場です。

正式な授業やレッスンではなく、サポートし合える環境の中で、自然な会話と心地よい言語練習を楽しむことを目的としています。日本語・英語ともに、初心者からネイティブまで、すべてのレベルの方を歓迎します。たくさん話しても、聞くだけでも大丈夫。自分のペースでご参加ください。

こんな方におすすめ

どなたでもご参加いただけます。特に次のような方におすすめです：

気軽な雰囲気で日本語または英語を練習したい方

日本、アメリカ、テキサス、オースティン、その他の文化に興味がある方

勉強というよりリラックスした会話を楽しみたい方

話すことに不安があるけれど、安全な環境で学びたい方

こんな時間が期待できます

フレンドリーで安心できる学習環境

自分のペースで会話を楽しむ時間

参加者同士の励ましとサポート

文化交流と言語学習の共有体験

話す準備ができていなくても、聞くだけでの参加も歓迎です！

学ぶペースは人それぞれ。思いやり、忍耐、そして支え合いが、このコミュニティの魅力です。

スケジュール（予定）

毎週火曜日　午後6時から8時

6:00–6:30 PM　チェックインと自由会話
6:30–7:00 PM　英語での会話
7:00–7:30 PM　日本語での会話
7:30 PM 後　自由会話（英語・日本語どちらでも）

※途中参加・途中退席も可能です。

ひとこと

初参加の方も多くいらっしゃいます。準備は不要ですので、どうぞ気軽にお越しください。

本イベントは参加無料ですが、会場へのご協力として、ミートアップ中にフードまたはドリンクのご注文をお願いいたします。皆さまのご支援が、このようなコミュニティイベントの継続につながります。

注意事項

参加登録：会場定員の都合上、事前登録が必要です。事前登録無しで参加される方に当日Eメールアドレスなどの提供をお願いすることがあります。個人情報の取り扱いについては、AJCのプライバシーポリシーが適用されます。あらかじめご了承ください。

キャンセル：予定が変更になった場合は、イベントページからキャンセル手続きをお願いします。

写真撮影：イベント中に撮影した写真をAJCのウェブサイトやSNSで使用する場合があります。写りたくない方は事前にスタッフへお知らせください。

安全について：イベント中の盗難・怪我・事故等について、AJCは責任を負いかねます。

勧誘・販売行為の禁止：無断での営業・勧誘行為は禁止です。安全で快適な環境を損なう行為があった場合、退場や今後の参加をお断りすることがあります。

ぺらぺらナイトで皆さまにお会いできるのを楽しみにしています！

質問等ありましたら、担当のJanまでご連絡ください。

events.title: The Health Hustlers: An Exclusive Networking Event for Health/Wellness Professionals
events.description:
Join us for an evening of meaningful connection and collaboration for anyone who works in the health, wellness, or fitness space here in Austin.

Whether you’re part of a wellness-focused business, contribute to a health-oriented team, or simply play an active role in helping others improve their well-being, this event is for you. Health Hustlers ATX is a space to build real relationships with people who understand your passion for helping others live better, healthier lives.

Who Should Attend:

Health, Wellness & Fitness Professionals

Anyone who works in the health, wellness, or fitness industry and wants to connect with others in the community. There’s no requirement to be an owner or founder — just someone actively involved in the field.

Growth-Oriented Individuals

You’re interested in collaborating, sharing ideas, learning from others, and expanding your circle within Austin’s wellness landscape.

Community-Driven People

You appreciate authentic connection over transactional networking and want to be part of a supportive, positive environment.

Please Note:
This is not a sales-driven or pitch-focused event. We encourage open conversation, genuine connection, and collaboration.

What to Expect:

A relaxed, welcoming environment that makes meeting new people easy

Meaningful conversations about health, wellness, and personal growth

Unique formats that encourage real connection, not awkward small talk

Opportunities to build long-term relationships and collaborations

Itinerary:

6:30–7:00 p.m. — Meet & Greet 🤝
7:00–7:45 p.m. — Small Group Icebreakers 🧊
7:45–8:00 p.m. — Speed Networking 🏃‍♂️
8:00 p.m. — Group Photo 📸
8:00-8:30 p.m. — Friendworking 🤠
8:30 p.m. — Goodbyes! 👋

Limited spots available. Please register in advance to secure your place.

We look forward to connecting with you and continuing to strengthen Austin’s wellness community together!

Reach out to Dr. Scott at drscott@limitlesschiroatx.com with any questions.

events.title: Beans & Builders Founder Meetup
events.description:
Beans & Builders is a relaxed, informal meetup put on by Austin Founders Feed, bringing local founders together to connect, swap stories, and learn from each other over some good coffee.

Real conversations about the wins, the problems, and everything in between. To keep things flowing, we'll have a few light prompts and topics on hand so the conversation never gets stale, covering Marketing & Growth, Product, Systems & AI, and Funding (bootstrapping vs. raising money).

Our goal is simple: get to know this community better and give founders a space to share what they're working on and what they're stuck on. Come as you are, grab a cup, and meet some of the people building in Austin.

The first few to arrive get a little something from us, so it pays to be early. See you there.

Beans & Builders is hosted by Austin Founders Feed, a free newsletter covering the founders, startups, and stories shaping Austin.

events.title: The Long Game: Artist Development, Community, & Career Sustainability
events.description:
The music industry often celebrates breakthroughs, viral moments, and success stories; but what does it actually take to build a sustainable career over the long term?

Join Prosper XO for an evening exploring artist development, community, career sustainability, building a business, and the support systems that help artists grow, evolve, and thrive.

From artist management and record labels to nonprofit support and community infrastructure, we'll discuss the realities of building a lasting career in music, the challenges artists face along the way, and what it takes to create an ecosystem where artists can succeed not just for a moment; but for a lifetime.

Whether you're an artist, manager, entrepreneur, student, industry professional, or passionate supporter of the arts, this conversation is for you.

📅 Wednesday, July 8th | 6–8:30 PM

This event is free and open to the public.

Featuring:

Justin Weems
Founder, FSG | FSG Records

Justin Weems is a founder of FSG, a Texas-based apparel brand, community, and creative movement that has grown into a platform supporting artists, culture, and independent expression. He is also a founder of FSG Records, where he works to develop artists, build sustainable careers, and create opportunities that extend beyond traditional industry models. Through both FSG and FSG Records, Justin has helped cultivate a community-driven approach to artist development rooted in authenticity, creativity, and long-term growth.

Jazz Mills
CEO, Free Lunch | Artist Manager | Artist

Jazz Mills is the CEO of Free Lunch, artist manager, and longtime performing artist whose career has given her firsthand experience navigating the realities of the music industry from multiple perspectives. Through her work as both an artist and advocate, she understands the challenges creatives face while building sustainable careers. Today, she works to support artists through management, community-building, education, and the creation of opportunities that help artists thrive both creatively and professionally.

Hosted by:

Lauren Bruno & Prosper XO

Prosper XO is an artist-first ethical technology company and education + cultural initiative focused on artist sustainability, creative autonomy, ownership, and community building. Through monthly Town Halls, Prosper XO brings together artists, industry leaders, educators, researchers, entrepreneurs, and community members to explore the future of creativity, culture, and artist prosperity.

At Prosper XO, we believe artists are essential cultural partners. We believe value created by the creative community should help strengthen the creative community. And we believe that when artists have access to the support, resources, opportunities, and relationships they need to thrive, entire communities benefit.

Expect networking, meaningful conversation, new connections, and a room full of people passionate about building a stronger future for artists.

Because success isn't built overnight.

And when artists prosper, culture thrives.

Logistical Details

Parking Discount: Park in the Omni Hotel garage below Capital Factory and pick up a voucher at the event for a reduced $8 fee.

Location: ACL Room – 16th Floor, Capital Factory
701 Brazos St, Austin, TX 78701

Special Thanks

Thank you to Station Austin for sponsoring Prosper XO. Station Austin is the center of gravity for entrepreneurs in Texas, connecting founders with investors, mentors, employees, and customers.

Thank you to Free Lunch for their partnership and continued commitment to supporting artists and creative communities.

Stay Connected

Join our mailing list for updates & future events:
https://mailchi.mp/d31733a7aa36/prosperxo

Want to speak at an upcoming Town Hall?
Want to sponsor a future event?
Email: lauren@prosperxo.com

Follow Prosper XO:
Instagram
LinkedIn 
X 
TikTok
YouTube

🌈💗✊🎶

events.title: Trail Talks #5
events.description:
Alright, we're back for a summer edition of Trail Talks. Yes, it's the week after July 4th, but let's be honest: the long weekend leaves a mark… and this is the perfect blend of "I'm still doing work" without having to sit behind a desk.

We're keeping our featured founder and investor under wraps until one week before the walk, so stay tuned for the reveal.

Moving forward, we're also going to be featuring a charity every week. This walk we'll hear from Andrea Olvera with CASA - an incredible group of volunteers that advocate for children who’ve experienced abuse or neglect as they navigate the court system.

As always, you'll be in great company: founders, investors, and startup folks who show up curious, generous, and ready to swap ideas.

Remember to bring your friends! We'll see you out there!

Sincerely,

Pat (https://lonestartup.com) & Sam (https://www.hill-climbers.com) (we do startup podcasts)

events.title: Writers Walk
events.description:
Get out of your chair and onto the trail!

Join us for a relaxed, social morning with ATX Writing Club as we meet up for coffee, then take a 3-mile walk around Town Lake. 

This is not a writing session. Instead we're giving you a chance to meet other members of the club, while soaking up the great outdoors!

We’ll grab some coffee and then do some quick introductions. Along the way, we’ll offer a few simple prompts to get conversation going (nothing heavy lol). Every so often, we’ll switch walking partners so you get a mix of perspectives and meet more people in the club.

Expect a steady pace, good coffee, and a rotating set of conversations with people who are serious about their creative lives (but don’t take themselves too seriously).

Schedule:

9:00am - Arrive and grab some coffee

9:15am - Do group intros and establish this weeks topic

9:20am - Head out for a walk!

10:30am - Walk done. Arrive back, exchange info

If you’ve been wanting to meet more members of ATX Writing Club, or just want a more social way to spend your morning, this is a solid place to start!

events.title: Chapter 2: Let’s Get Spicy | AnniversaryPoolside Reader Social
events.description:
Join us for our biggest reader social yet as we celebrate two years of Keep Austin Romantic!

Spend the evening connecting with local romance readers while enjoying a curated summer experience at a private luxury vacation rental in the heart of Travis Heights. Whether you’re swimming, lounging poolside with a book, crafting custom keepsakes, enjoying dinner, or simply making new friends, this event is designed to bring readers together beyond the page.

Whether it’s your first Keep Austin Romantic event or your fifteenth, we’d love to welcome you.

Your Ticket Includes

🌮 Catered Tex-Mex fajita buffet

🕶️ DIY sunglasses and keychain craft bar

📸 Professional photography throughout the evening

📚 Bookish games

🍓 Desserts & Prosecco popsicles

🎁 Goodie bag

🏊 Poolside fun

🧖 Sauna & cold plunge

📖 Reading nooks and indoor lounge spaces

The Venue

Celebrate with us at a beautiful private luxury vacation rental tucked away in the heart of Travis Heights.

Enjoy:

Resort-style pool & lounge areas

Sauna & cold plunge

Beautiful spaces & photo moments

Indoor & outdoor lounge areas

Swim, lounge, read, craft, and celebrate, all in one beautiful space.

🔒 The exact address and parking instructions will be emailed to ticket holders before the event.

What to Know

🩴 Bring: A towel, swimsuit, sunscreen, and your current read if you’d like to relax poolside.

🌮 Included: Catered fajita buffet, desserts, sparkling refreshments, water, and bubbly. BYOB is welcome!

❄️ Need a break from the heat? Plenty of beautiful indoor lounge space with A/C is available throughout the event.

💖 Whether it’s your first meetup or your fifteenth, we’d love to have you.

18+ ONLY

🎟️ Tickets

Early Bird — $55
Available June 26 through July 5 (or until sold out)

Standard Admission — $65
Beginning July 6

Attendance is intentionally limited so every guest has plenty of room to swim, lounge, craft, and enjoy the entire experience.

Special Thanks

A huge thank you to our incredible partners for helping bring this celebration to life.

🏡 Via Luxury Rentals for hosting us in one of their beautiful luxury vacation homes.

📸 Channing Stark Photography (@seeing_spirit_photog) for capturing all the memories from our anniversary celebration.

We can’t wait to celebrate the next chapter with you. ❤️

events.title: Austin | Claude Code Meetup
events.description:
Welcome to Claude Code Austin!

Thanks to everyone who has been coming out to our events!   

We're back with our July edition of Claude Code Austin. This meetup is created to help connect the local community and learn more about using Claude. Thank you to everyone who has provided great feedback on our past events.

6:00 PM - 6:25 PM - Doors Open & Networking
Grab some refreshments and connect with local builders.

6:25 PM - 6:30 PM - Event Welcome

6:30 PM - 7:10 PM - Presenters
We'll hear from 3 builders showcasing projects they've created using Claude Code. If you're interested in presenting please complete this form.
Note we have limited slots available for demo presentations. We give high consideration to any demo that helps our attendees learn tactics for building with Claude Code.

7:10 PM - 8:00 PM - Breakout Sessions 
Our group will break out in smaller volunteer led sessions on specific topics related to Claude Code. If you're interested in leading a breakout session please complete this form. 

8:00 PM - Wrap Up and Closing
Final wrap up and closing.

A special thank you to Station Austin for sponsoring Claude Code Meetup Austin. STATION Austin is the center of gravity for entrepreneurs in Texas. Day and night, in-person and online, we gather the best founders, programmers, and designers outside of Silicon Valley and introduce them to investors, employees, and customers who help their ideas launch. STATION Austin is powered by Capital Factory, whose investments and leadership have helped power the Texas startup ecosystem for more than a decade. To sign up for a STATION Austin membership, click here.

events.title: AI that works: Apify + TwelveLabs
events.description:
AI that works is a monthly meetup for anyone building with AI in Austin: developers, founders, operators, go-to-market (GTM) teams, and the AI-curious. Every session: live demos from people shipping real things, a hands-on build, and honest conversation about what's actually producing results.

We don't care if you're writing code or mostly prompting. If you're putting AI to work on something real, you belong here.

This month

We'll build a competitor ad-spy tool that turns hours of manual scrolling through the Meta Ad Library into a paste-one-URL workflow. You give it a competitor's Facebook page, and it pulls their video ads, ranks them by the spend and longevity signals that reveal a likely winner, and has AI watch each one to reverse-engineer the creative strategy: hook, offer, proof, and call to action (CTA).

If you run paid social, you'll leave knowing how to surface a competitor's winning ads and the structure behind them. If you build with AI, you'll leave with the full stack: an Apify Actor that orchestrates a scraper, a scoring model, and four different AI models into one tool.

How the evening works

We start with why studying competitor ads by hand doesn't work: you scroll the Ad Library, watch dozens of videos one at a time, screenshot hooks into a doc you never reopen, and you're still guessing which ads are actually winning. Then we build the tool that does it for you.

First, the data layer. We wrap apify/facebook-ads-scraper inside a single new Actor that pulls up to 100 ads per page with public metadata - active status, run duration, placements, variants, and spend signals - then scores and ranks them so the high-conviction ads float to the top.

Next, the analysis layer. We pass the top-ranked videos to TwelveLabs: Pegasus 1.5 returns a structured creative blueprint (hook, audience, problem framing, offer, proof, CTA, pacing, and scene-by-scene notes), and Marengo 3.0 indexes each video so you can query it later. Gemini then synthesizes a cross-competitor report on the patterns across hooks, visual styles, offers, and CTAs.

Finally, the interrogation layer. We wire up a split-screen video-and-chat view powered by Claude Sonnet, ask it "show me the hook" or "where's the CTA?", and click any returned timestamp to seek the video straight to that frame.

You'll leave with a working composite Actor in your Apify account and a repeatable pattern for orchestrating multiple models behind one clean input and one useful output.

Bring your laptop.

Agenda

5:30 pm - doors open and networking

5:45 pm - kickoff and presentation

6:00 pm - workshop

7:15 pm - wrap-up and chatting

7:30 pm - end

What you'll need for the hands-on portion

A laptop (macOS, Windows, or Linux)

An Apify account (free tier or higher) with enough platform usage left in the month to run workflows live

Node.js installed

Your preferred code editor (Cursor, VS Code, or similar)

Some comfort with the terminal and basic JavaScript/TypeScript - you don't need to be an expert, but you'll be editing code during the build

Don't have all of this? No worries, you can follow along with someone at the event who does. The concepts apply regardless of your stack.

About the host

Apify is the platform behind thousands of ready-to-use tools that give AI agents and apps access to web data, automation, and real-world information. But this meetup isn't a product pitch, it's a community. See what people are building, learn something new, and meet the people doing the work.

👾 Join the conversation on Discord.

Explore Apify at apify.com.

We will use your information to contact you about the event. For more information, see our Privacy Policy.

events.title: Antler's Summer Bash and Hot Ones Challenge with Rho!
events.description:
🔥 Beat the heat (or don't) at Antler's Summer Bash!

Join us for good food, drinks, and great conversation. The main event? A spicy twist on networking, our Hot Ones Challenge. Founders earn the chance to grill our very own, Jake O'Shea, one fiery wing at a time.

🌶️ The Hot Ones Challenge:

Here's the deal, founders get to put Jake in the hot seat, no question off-limits, but there's a catch. To earn your question, you've got to eat a wing at the same heat level. We'll climb the Scoville scale, starting mild and ending with a finale that hits 8 MILLION SHU. (For reference, that's well past pure chili extract territory and into "are you sure about this?" range.) The hotter the wing, the bolder the question.

Whether you're a founder, builder, operator, or just getting your startup off the ground, come connect with the people building alongside you.

Schedule

5:00-8:00 PM - Networking

6:00-7:00 PM - Hot Ones Challenge

Bring your toughest questions and your highest spice tolerance. See you there! 🔥

Meet your Hosts: 

Antler: 

Antler is an early-stage venture capital firm with a focus on inception stage founders and a community of resident tech entrepreneurs and operators. Founders are immersed in a community with everything they need to build and scale at lightning speed. If you're building, we want to hear about it!

Rho

From two-person startups to public companies, ​​​Rho is the banking platform with everything businesses need to manage cash, control spend, and automate finance busywork. Rho offers corporate cards, banking, treasury, expense management, AP, accounting automation, and more in one integrated platform backed by award-winning support.

Rho is a fintech company, not a bank. Checking and card services provided by Webster Bank, N.A., member FDIC; savings account services provided by American Deposit Management, LLC, and its partner banks.

events.title: LAUNCH PAD
events.description:
Launch Pad is where Austin’s startup community comes to see what’s next.

Hosted at STATION Austin, this pitch and networking event brings together founders, builders, investors, and innovation leaders for an evening of big ideas, rapid-fire pitches, and real connections across the ecosystem.

Attendees will hear from entrepreneurs and emerging companies as they share what they’re building, what problem they’re solving, and why it matters. Whether you’re looking for your next collaborator, customer, investment, or inspiration, Launch Pad is built to help you plug into the community and meet the people shaping what’s next in Texas startups.

This event is free and open to anyone interested in the innovation ecosystem.

For those ready to pitch, Launch Pad also gives founders and aspiring entrepreneurs a chance to take the mic and compete for prizes:

Grand Prize: 3-month coworking membership

Runner-Up: 1-month coworking membership

We’ll also have light bites and drinks, with updated food offerings designed to create a better networking experience. Need a headshot? We've got you!

Interested in pitching? Email memberships@stationaustin.org

events.title: Austin Female Founders & Friends
events.description:
Join The Artemis Fund for our quarterly coffee meet up bringing together Austin's female founder community. Anyone who could benefit from this network is welcome to attend.

The Artemis Fund leads seed rounds for tech companies building solutions to create resilient families, individuals, and businesses in care, commerce, future of work, and fintech. Artemis is currently deploying Fund II, backed by institutional LPs including Bank of America, BMO, TIAA Nuveen, Amazon, and the Commonwealth of Virginia.

Pilot is modernizing outsourced accounting, tax, fractional CFO, and COO services for startups & small businesses.

Disclaimer: Please be advised that by entering this event site you are agreeing to being filmed and/or photographed, and the resulting assets may be used for marketing or promotional purposes.
events.calendarName: The Artemis Fund - Female Founders Coffee

events.title: AITX Community x NVIDIA Claw Agent Hackathon
events.description:
AITX Community is partnering with NVIDIA for a hands-on Claw Agents hackathon focused on building real autonomous agent applications. Participants will build agents that do more than chat: they should take action, coordinate tools, and produce a working demo by the end of the event.

This is built for AI engineers, builders, founders, researchers, and technical operators who want to experiment with the next generation of agentic systems.

What you’ll build

Teams will build a working autonomous agent application. Strong projects will show:

A real user or enterprise workflow

Multi-step autonomous execution

Tool use, MCP integration, or API orchestration

Thoughtful handling of permissions, privacy, and security

A live demo that proves the agent can actually do the work

Potential project directions

Personal AI operators that manage complex recurring workflows

Secure enterprise agents for internal tools, data, and operations

Developer agents that inspect repos, open PRs, run tests, or manage issues

Research agents that gather, verify, and synthesize information

Local-first or privacy-aware agents that minimize unnecessary cloud exposure

Multi-agent systems that divide work across specialized roles

Who should apply?

This event is ideal for builders with experience in AI engineering, full-stack development, infrastructure, automation, agents, open-source tooling, or applied ML. You do not need to be an expert in building Agents before arriving, but you should be comfortable learning quickly and shipping a demo.

Why attend?

You’ll get hands-on time with emerging NVIDIA agent infrastructure, meet other serious AI builders in Austin, and build alongside a community focused on practical, technically ambitious AI applications.

Expect a fast-moving builder environment, technical support, team formation, sponsor resources, live demos, and prizes for the strongest projects.

Their support makes this Hackathon possible.

Red Hat AI

Red Hat AI is a big player in the open-source AI ecosystem. It actively contributes to numerous projects, including InstructLab, which enables fine-tuning of large language models (LLMs) on consumer-grade hardware, and vLLM, a library designed for efficient LLM inference and model serving. Additionally, Red Hat offers an enterprise-grade AI platform that supports end-to-end development of AI-powered applications.

HiddenLayer

HiddenLayer is a leading AI security company focused on protecting machine learning models, generative AI applications, and agentic systems from emerging threats. HiddenLayer is built for organizations deploying AI at scale that need to secure models, prevent misuse, protect intellectual property, and maintain compliance without slowing down AI adoption.

events.title: Flippin' Fundamentals
events.description:
In this hands-on, glass-off class, you'll learn the basics of controlling the ball, knowing what to shoot for and how to make the shot. We'll cover game rules, nudging, and introduce you to some advanced techniques like the post pass, slap save and live catch.

Cost: $20 (at the door). Includes a same-day pass to the collective so you can practice your skills!

events.title: Reverse Brain Rot
events.description:
Consider this your cognitive gym

Social media is making us dumber, more depressed, and more anxious.

AI is degrading our ability to think critically, come up with new ideas, and express our individuality.

These are the two culprits of brain rot.

How this event works

1 hour of reading

1 hour of writing

No screens allowed

It's really that simple. Just a little bit of daily focused reading and writing, can reverse years of brain rot.

NOTE:

Bring whatever kind of reading you'd like. It could be a book, a short story, some poems, or even articles you printed out from online.

You can also write about whatever you like. It can be a letter to your mom, a dissertation, or a reflection on the works you just read. It doesn't have to be professional, and it doesn't even have to be good.

This is a cognitive gym, which means any effort is good effort.

This event is a collaboration with Nicole Stump, writer of Stumped by Nature. Subscribe for a weekly dose of local nature + curated outside events.

events.title: Austin Reading Club
events.description:
Welcome to Austin Reading Club! 

This is not a book club in the traditional sense.

You'll bring a book of your choice.

Other readers will do the same.

And we'll read together, separately.

Our itinerary:

6:00pm Hellos to our fellow readers

6:15pm Sink into your book for reading time

7:15pm Touch base with society again

FAQs:

Are we really just reading in the same room silently together?  
Yes. And we get to socialize at the top and bottom of that reading time.

Can I read something other than a book? 
Sure. You're an adult (must be over age 21 for this venue). Take care of your intellectual needs as you see fit. 

Will you have books to borrow? 
Some readers may have a spare to share, but your best bet is to bring something from your TBR list.

Do audiobooks count? 
If you can listen without distracting your physical-book-peers, you do you.

events.title: AI Workflow Night | AustinJS Meetup
events.description:
Join us for a special AustinJS night dedicated entirely to AI Workflows!

On this AustinJS open mic night, come and share how you've been interacting with AI tools and workflows. Don't know what to talk about but want to participate? Here are a couple of ideas:

Building with AI (Product & Tech): Built an autonomous agent, deployed an LLM-powered feature, or shipped a clever AI workflow? Show us the tech stack, the architecture, and how it works under the hood.

Building Faster with AI (Dev Productivity): Developed a killer prompt workflow, paired up with local models, or customized your IDE to write code at warp speed? Pull up a terminal and show us your daily setup.

Meetup Schedule:
6:00 - 7:00 -> Food and networking
7:00 - 8:00 -> AI Workflow Presentations
8:00 -> Walk to Zilker Brewing Company and hangout

Come and give a talk! We'd also love to have you come give a lightning presentation or present a battle deck!

events.title: Out in Tech Austin: July Mixer
events.description:
Out in Tech brings together LGBTQ+ professionals and allies across tech, startups, and digital culture. Join the Out in Tech ATX community for our monthly mixer at Cheer Up Charlies.

This event is all about showing up as yourself, meeting cool people, and building community. Whether you work in tech, are tech-adjacent, or just curious, come grab a drink, spark a conversation, and connect with folks across roles, companies, and experience levels. No panels. No pitches. Just good vibes and great people.

events.title: 🎨 Watercolor Workshop - Spirited Away [Stargazer]
events.description:
The iconic train scene 🚂✨

We’re heading to the spirit world for an evening of painting, hanging out, and food/drinks. Beginner friendly!

Another opportunity to join our Spirited Away workshop :)

ABOUT

Paint Chihiro, No-Face, and Haku Thursday night, bring a friend, or just come to vibe and meet new people.

What’s included in your ticket: 

Guided instructions

Template - line art to paint

All supplies provided

A finished 8x10 painting that's ready to frame

Food & drinks are separate, but Hold Out’s menu is oishii, so come hungry/thirsty.

🎨 Monthly paint nights at Hold Out Brewing

What we'll be painting using line template:

Photos from our last Spirited Away Event

🕒 Itinerary

7:00 PM – 7:15 PM | Arrive settle into your station, play with supplies, and grab a craft brew/snack before we start

7:15 PM – 7:45 PM | Watercolor basics live demo, follow along!

7:45 PM – 8:45 PM | painting the main event. chat & create. I'll walk around to help!

8:45 PM – 9:00 PM | share your art see / view creations

FAQ

Are there ADA Accommodations?

Please reach out if you need ADA Accommodations: hellosarahtdoan@gmail.com

Where should I meet?

At tables in front next to bar.

What if I'm late?

I can catch you up on what you missed after I've complete the instructions :)

Is there food?

Stargazer has drinks and food trucks outside!

Follow on IG @GetCrafty.ATX for upcoming events, getcraftyatx.com

Paint and Sip, Creative Classes, Austin Events, Art Class, Social Gathering, Ghibli Aesthetic, Anime Art, ATX Events, Things to do in Austin, Austin Creatives

events.title: OFFTRACK hosted by TRACKRAT
events.description:
OFFTRACK by TRACKRAT ⚡️

An evening to showcase cool and helpful projects.

Join us for OFFTRACK, a casual evening from the TRACKRAT community to showcase the cool stuff they've built with technology.

Unlike your typical networking events, OFFTRACK is a small, once-every-couple-months event put on by TRACKRAT Sprint Club. 

Demos are 5-10 minutes each.

Format

🎤 4–5 presenters

⏱️ 5-10 minute demo or presentation

❓ 5-minute Q&A after each session

Whether you're building an AI startup, experimenting with side projects, or simply curious about what's possible, come for a night of show and learn, great conversations, and inspiration.

🍕 Food & drinks provided
🤝 Meet builders, creators, founders, and AI enthusiasts.

More details coming soon. 🚀

RSVP - Limited Seats

TRACKRAT was started by founders, investors, and operators building in tech. The track taught us something simple: you get better, faster, around people who push you. OFFTRACK takes that same energy off the track and into the things we make.

Learn more at: www.trackratsprint.club

events.title: The Consumer VC Wake Up! CPG Austin
events.description:
​Austin-area CPG brands, retailers, service providers, and local enthusiasts are welcome to join us for a lively discussion about the CPG scene and helping each other grow our businesses and community. We are especially welcoming to new startups and companies just landing in Austin.

​Wake Up! is a casual networking meetup for people interested in the Austin Consumer Packaged Goods Community. This event is hosted by Marc Nathan with generous sponsorship from Michael Best & Friedrich, LLP and EventMagic.

--

Nathan Latka (https://www.linkedin.com/in/nathanlatka/) is graciously covering everyone's coffee for our 2026 Wake Up! CPG Kickoff! He's the most active CPG investor in Austin and he'll invest $50m in Austin CPG brands this year. He already invested in Pecan Cakes, Parker & Scott, and Gordoughs Donuts to name a few: https://founderpath.com/industry/cpg

Nathan will be joining us on Thursday along with some special out-of-town guests, including Amy Noblin (https://www.linkedin.com/in/amy-noblin-64aaa26/), visiting from Colorado.

Watch Nathan's excellent web series, The Deal here: https://www.youtube.com/@DealShowClips?sub_confirmation=1
